hero_subtitle: SFTY connects and consolidates sanctions, PEP, and adverse media outputs into one structured workflow to reduce manual reviews and strengthen audit readiness for digital asset teams.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Screening results remain inconsistent across providers
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle with disjointed sanctions, PEP and adverse media outputs that create duplicated efforts and unclear risk context. This fragmentation slows decision-making and weakens the audit trail needed for defensible reviews and regulatory reporting.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY consolidates sanctions, PEP and adverse media outputs from multiple providers into a unified review workflow that preserves decision evidence and accelerates consistent, auditable compliance operations.
workflow_title: From screening results to normalized alerts
workflow_description: SFTY collects and normalizes screening results from multiple providers then routes alerts into consistent reviewable cases with audit evidence and reporting.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond fragmented outputs. Structured screening control
why_sfty_description: SFTY turns fragmented screening results from multiple providers into normalized workflows, cases, decisions and audit-ready evidence for clearer compliance control.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASPs with controlled provider integration and audit-ready evidence to support consistent screening results and clearer compliance during due diligence and deployment.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY normalize screening results across providers
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how screening outputs become audit-ready evidence
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how SFTY consolidates sanctions and PEP screening results
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe how normalized data supports case review workflows
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for screening operations?
faq_1_answer: SFTY centralizes sanctions, PEP and adverse media screening results into one compliance workflow for VASP screening operations, reducing fragmentation and improving operational oversight.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY normalize screening results?
faq_2_answer: SFTY aggregates and normalizes outputs from multiple screening providers, turning varied results into connected alerts, structured cases and consistent review workflows for operational clarity.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves screening evidence, decision logs and case history with timestamps and reviewer actions to support review readiness and audit traceability.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate existing screening providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY connects with existing sanctions, PEP and adverse media screening providers, orchestrating their outputs without disrupting current compliance stacks.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace sanctions or PEP providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace screening providers but layers their outputs into structured workflows, evidence and reports for clearer compliance control.
final_title: Bring screening results into controlled workflows
final_description: Review how SFTY normalizes sanctions, PEP and adverse media outputs into structured cases and audit-ready evidence.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
